issues.description: FR-6 conflicts with current config command architecture. `nemo config` is dispatched BEFORE config loading (main.rs:788) to allow repairing broken configs. FR-6a/FR-6b require profile-aware writes (`--set` writes to active profile, `--profile=<name>` writes to named profile), but the config command path currently bypasses the main config/flag resolution flow. The spec doesn't address how `nemo config --set` should handle migration (what if config is still flat when --set is called?) or how `--profile` global flag is threaded into the early-dispatch path.
issues.suggestion: Add an implementation note: the config command must perform migration first (FR-2a) before any --set, and must resolve the --profile flag independently since it runs before the main flag resolution. Specify behavior for `nemo config --set server_url=X` when config is still in flat (pre-migration) format.

issues.description: FR-6d shows `api_key: String` (required) in ProfileConfig, but the current config has `api_key: Option<String>`. FR-3b says `--api-key` is required for `profile add`, but the existing config allows `api_key` to be None (with a bail at runtime). After migration, a config with no api_key would produce a ProfileConfig with an empty or missing api_key. The spec doesn't clarify whether api_key should remain Optional in ProfileConfig to match current behavior where it can be absent.
issues.suggestion: Make `api_key` an `Option<String>` in ProfileConfig to match current behavior, OR explicitly state that migration should fail/warn if api_key is missing. Also clarify: if a user runs `nemo profile add` with `--api-key`, is it validated non-empty?

issues.description: FR-6c introduces support for `helm.*` and `models.*` keys in `nemo config --set`, but the current implementation only supports flat keys (server_url, engineer, name, email, api_key). The spec frames this as 'continues writing to the root section' implying existing behavior, but it's actually new functionality. The spec doesn't specify the syntax for nested keys (dot notation? e.g., `helm.desktop_notifications=true`) or how to parse the value types (boolean vs string).
issues.suggestion: Explicitly call out that `helm.*` and `models.*` support in `--set` is NEW behavior (not a continuation). Specify the dot-notation parsing, value type coercion (e.g., 'true'/'false' → bool), and which specific `helm.*` and `models.*` sub-keys are valid.

issues.description: FR-2a migration says 'first-run of ANY nemo command that would touch config' triggers migration, but `nemo config` is dispatched before config loading and has its own load path. Commands like `nemo help`, `nemo capabilities`, and `nemo init` also bypass config loading. The spec doesn't precisely define which commands trigger migration vs. which don't.
issues.suggestion: Clarify: migration triggers on any command that calls `load_config()` in the normal flow (i.e., all commands except help, capabilities, init). Specify that `nemo config` should also trigger migration before processing --set/--get.

issues.description: FR-3a says `nemo profile rm <name>` errors if `<name>` is the active one, but doesn't specify behavior when removing the last remaining profile. An engineer could have two profiles, switch to profile B, remove profile A — leaving only B. Then they can't remove B because it's active. This is fine, but what about `nemo profile rm` when there's only one non-active profile? Not really an edge case but the 'cannot remove active' + no minimum profile count means a user could get stuck with a stale profile they can't remove.
issues.suggestion: This is actually fine as-is (rename or create new + switch + remove old). Consider adding a brief note that workaround for removing the last profile is to create a new one, switch to it, then remove the old one — or just document that at least one profile must always exist.

issues.description: FR-3a defines `nemo profile rename <old> <new>` but doesn't specify validation: should <new> be validated against the same regex as FR-1b? Should it error if <new> already exists?
issues.suggestion: Add: '<new> must satisfy the FR-1b name regex. Error if a profile named <new> already exists.'

issues.description: FR-5a specifies a header line format for `nemo status` but doesn't specify behavior when using `--profile` override vs. `current_profile`. Should the header show the effective profile name regardless of source?
issues.suggestion: Clarify: header always shows the effective profile name (after precedence resolution), regardless of whether it came from --profile flag, env var, or current_profile.

issues.description: FR-1a shows `name` and `email` as optional in the example (dev profile omits them) but FR-6d struct shows them as `Option<String>`. FR-3b says `--name` and `--email` default to current profile's values. The interaction is clear but could be more explicit: what happens when the first profile (from migration) has empty name/email, and a new profile is added without --name/--email?
issues.suggestion: Minor: add a note that if the current profile's name/email are empty, the defaults for a new profile's --name/--email are also empty (not an error).

issues.description: The spec mentions `nemo use-profile <name>` as a top-level command AND `nemo profile use` as an alias, but the command table in FR-3a lists it under the profile subcommand group. The dual registration (both top-level and sub-command) should be explicit about implementation approach with clap.
issues.suggestion: Add implementation note: register `use-profile` as a top-level Commands variant and `use` as a Profile subcommand variant, both routing to the same handler.
